title: IR Night-Vision Camera Module
design_question: How to enable video surveillance in zero-light environments?
selected_approach: Board camera with 1/3" CMOS sensor and onboard 850 nm IR LED array (6–12 LEDs, 5–10 m night range). Composite or USB-UVC output; pairs with an FPV transmitter or USB capture card on the control station.
rationale: 850 nm IR is invisible to human eye yet clearly detected by the sensor. All-in-one camera+LED board costs < £10. Suitable for spy/patrol robots and night patrol drones.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Detects heat through darkness—no IR illumination needed but lower resolution.
Usable at 0.001 lux without IR illumination but 5× cost.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Infrared Cliff Detection for Robot Vacuums' confirms 850 nm IR reflectance detection principle.
supporting_fact_titles: Infrared Cliff Detection for Robot Vacuums
